content: Just as guided explicit vocabulary reflection in high school English learning helps learners refine inaccurate word usage and internalize linguistic patterns by confronting gaps between their output and standard usage, LLM self-reflection frameworks like Self-Contrast can borrow this structured language pedagogy design to build graded reflection curricula for large language models that improve reasoning ability. This turns the abstract technical concept of LLM self-reflection into a structured learning process analogous to second language vocabulary acquisition.
